stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under the 'text-generation' pipeline, indicating its primary function.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
The attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, impacting safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating an ongoing optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
stories.author: Amber Dance, Knowable Magazine
bullet_points: CAR T cell therapy is being investigated for treating autoimmune diseases.
The therapy aims to 'reset' the immune system, a significant biological intervention.
The research represents a potential paradigm shift in cancer and autoimmune treatment.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively using AI to combat insider trading.
The focus of the AI application is specifically on monitoring prediction markets for illicit trading activity.
This signals a significant regulatory shift towards leveraging advanced machine learning for financial market integrity.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a high-stakes copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The settlement's approval is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the deal to secure large fees.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are deemed insufficient to verify complex safety claims demanded by current AI governance frameworks.
Governance requires verifiable evidence of properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited by what they can observe from model outputs.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Identifies a systematic dual failure where gradient-based unlearning methods lose efficacy under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
Proposes 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' as a necessary advancement to ensure model forgetting persists in real-world, compressed deployments.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective threat coverage of existing LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in the STRIDE threat model, derived from 932 arXiv security studies.
This methodology allows for benchmark-external validation, assessing the overall security coverage rather than just individual benchmark quality.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source model deployment (Ollama) and proprietary model capabilities (Anthropic).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled a lawsuit concerning the impact of social media on students.
The suit, filed by a Kentucky school district, alleged that social media addiction has caused learning disruption and a mental health crisis.
The legal action highlights growing concerns among educational institutions regarding the financial and academic strain caused by social media use.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US, China, and Russia are actively monitoring or targeting satellites in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
The article suggests that satellites are not as stealthy as previously assumed, as they are visible against the blackness of space.
The geopolitical tension in space is escalating, moving beyond traditional silent operations.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge of maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) over long video sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more rigorous evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is the ability to replay real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with evolving information.
This allows agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while maintaining temporal realism.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for 3D scene editing that operates natively in a feed-forward manner.
It addresses a key limitation in current 3D reconstruction models: their inability to respond dynamically to human instructions.
The research moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies used in existing scene editing methods.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generated video from world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide objective diagnostics for geometric failures in video generation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and unstable optimization as bottlenecks when co-training disparate PDE regimes.
Proposes using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands from different physics simulations.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: OpenDeepThink addresses the selection bottleneck in parallel LLM reasoning by using a population-based framework.
The method leverages pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best reasoning candidate without relying on noisy pointwise judging.
It proposes scaling LLM reasoning breadth by sampling multiple candidates in parallel, moving beyond traditional single-trace depth scaling.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image screening models, specifically lack of interpretability and historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical diagnosis.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative richness and temporal accuracy.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses visual reasoning in AI, contrasting two primary approaches: agentic and latent reasoning.
Agentic methods rely on external execution (like code/tool calls) but suffer from context-switching latency.
Latent reasoning uses learnable hidden embeddings as an alternative to computationally expensive direct image generation.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new framework for testing AI agents in dynamic environments.
The simulation replays real-world events chronologically to provide realistic testing scenarios.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with historical data.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for systematic comparison in agentic search systems, specifically how retrieval strategy interacts with agent architecture.
It focuses on the limitations of current Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) implementations within complex LLM agent workflows.
The research explores the interplay between retrieval methods and tool-calling paradigms in autonomous AI agents.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of temporal precision in clinical data, a critical issue for patient trajectory modeling (e.g., sepsis).
It proposes a multimodal approach combining unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The core methodology involves Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to reconstruct accurate clinical timelines.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely applications.
Encodes new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model for efficient knowledge incorporation.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current evaluations where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is currently underrepresented in research.

stories.title: I Added Three Rules to Gemma 4. The MoE Searched. The Dense Model Refused.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: The article compares the performance of Gemma 4 (MoE vs. Dense) against GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini in a real-world Arabic e-commerce chatbot scenario.
A key finding suggests that adding specific Gemma-only prompt rules caused divergent behavior: the MoE variant succeeded while the dense variant failed.
This highlights architectural differences (MoE vs. Dense) in how LLMs handle complex, real-world tasks, especially when constrained by specific prompting.

stories.title: Building an AI Chat Starter Kit for CMP: ~20 Lines from Empty Screen to ChatGPT-Quality Streaming
stories.author: nadeemiqbal
bullet_points: Open-sourced a starter kit for building AI chat applications.
Utilizes Compose Multiplatform for cross-platform development.
Achieves ChatGPT-quality streaming functionality with minimal code (~20 lines).
